top_findings.title: Known Bad IP Communication
top_findings.recommendation: Block the destination IP immediately and perform retrospective searches across related logs and endpoints.

notes: Campaign correlation completed across bundle-level investigation results

campaign_findings.title: Suspected Long-Running Malicious Infrastructure
campaign_findings.description: The entity `10.128.239.57` persisted across multiple bundles and hosts, suggesting campaign-level malicious activity rather than an isolated event.
campaign_findings.recommendation: Perform retrospective scoping across all affected hosts, block associated infrastructure, and validate whether this activity represents sustained intrusion or command-and-control.

hypothesis_timeline.title: External Sensitive Access
hypothesis_timeline.description: External IP accessed internal host on sensitive port, suggesting unauthorized remote access.
false_positive_risks: Legitimate remote administration via RDP or SSH from authorized external IPs.
VPN or jump-host traffic may appear as external access.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between authorized and unauthorized remote access without credential context.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Potential Data Exfiltration
hypothesis_timeline.description: Large or frequent outbound transfers to external host suggest data exfiltration.
false_positive_risks: Large legitimate uploads (backups, cloud sync, CI/CD) may trigger volumetric thresholds.
limitations: Volumetric analysis detects transfer patterns, not content — payload inspection requires decryption.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Potential Data Exfiltration
hypothesis_timeline.description: Large or frequent outbound transfers to external host suggest data exfiltration.
false_positive_risks: Large legitimate uploads (backups, cloud sync, CI/CD) may trigger volumetric thresholds.
limitations: Volumetric analysis detects transfer patterns, not content — payload inspection requires decryption.

hypothesis_timeline.title: External Sensitive Access
hypothesis_timeline.description: External IP accessed internal host on sensitive port, suggesting unauthorized remote access.
false_positive_risks: Legitimate remote administration via RDP or SSH from authorized external IPs.
VPN or jump-host traffic may appear as external access.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between authorized and unauthorized remote access without credential context.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Suspicious TLS Session
hypothesis_timeline.description: Suspicious TLS metadata suggests encrypted malicious communication.
false_positive_risks: Missing SNI may occur in privacy-focused or legacy environments and is not inherently malicious.
TLS on non-standard ports is suspicious but can still be legitimate.
limitations: TLS analysis may be constrained by unavailable JA3, limited SNI visibility, or incomplete handshake metadata.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Multi-Signal Threat
hypothesis_timeline.description: Multiple suspicious communication patterns from the same host suggest coordinated malicious activity.
false_positive_risks: A host with multiple benign anomalies may still be flagged by correlation.
limitations: Correlation strength depends on the quality and coverage of upstream analyzers.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Suspicious DNS Activity
hypothesis_timeline.description: High-entropy or unusually structured DNS queries suggest possible algorithmic domains, covert DNS use, or DNS-based command-and-control. Additional corroboration is required before classifying as tunneling.
false_positive_risks: High-entropy DNS can also appear in CDNs, telemetry, security products, and benign service-generated domains.
Repeated subdomain variation is suspicious but does not alone prove DNS tunneling.
limitations: DNS classification relies on metadata and naming patterns rather than payload semantics.

false_positive_risks: Missing SNI may occur in privacy-focused or legacy environments and is not inherently malicious.
TLS on non-standard ports is suspicious but can still be legitimate.
limitations: TLS analysis may be constrained by unavailable JA3, limited SNI visibility, or incomplete handshake metadata.

hypothesis_timeline.title: External Sensitive Access
hypothesis_timeline.description: External IP accessed internal host on sensitive port, suggesting unauthorized remote access.
false_positive_risks: Legitimate remote administration via RDP or SSH from authorized external IPs.
VPN or jump-host traffic may appear as external access.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between authorized and unauthorized remote access without credential context.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Potential Data Exfiltration
hypothesis_timeline.description: Large or frequent outbound transfers to external host suggest data exfiltration.
false_positive_risks: Large legitimate uploads (backups, cloud sync, CI/CD) may trigger volumetric thresholds.
limitations: Volumetric analysis detects transfer patterns, not content — payload inspection requires decryption.

hypothesis_timeline.title: External Sensitive Access
hypothesis_timeline.description: External IP accessed internal host on sensitive port, suggesting unauthorized remote access.
false_positive_risks: Legitimate remote administration via RDP or SSH from authorized external IPs.
VPN or jump-host traffic may appear as external access.
limitations: Cannot distinguish between authorized and unauthorized remote access without credential context.

hypothesis_timeline.title: Multi-Signal Threat
hypothesis_timeline.description: Multiple suspicious communication patterns from the same host suggest coordinated malicious activity.
false_positive_risks: A host with multiple benign anomalies may still be flagged by correlation.
limitations: Correlation strength depends on the quality and coverage of upstream analyzers.

executive_summary: The master synthesizer reviewed 52 bundle-level reports and consolidated 83 reportable findings. The most recurrent finding categories were External Sensitive Access, Suspicious TLS Session, Potential Data Exfiltration. The most frequently affected entities included 88.214.25.115->10.128.239.57:3389, 194.165.17.11->10.128.239.57:3389, 91.238.181.7->10.128.239.57:3389, 91.238.181.10->10.128.239.57:3389, 91.238.181.8->10.128.239.57:3389, and the most recurrent source hosts included 10.128.239.57, 88.214.25.115, 194.165.17.11, 91.238.181.7, 91.238.181.10. Cross-bundle correlation identified 10 campaign-level finding(s), indicating persistence beyond a single bundle.
